selected_problem_solved:
The proof establishes the following conditional result: on \(E=C_0([0,1])\), if \(P_{t_0}^n(x,\cdot)\Rightarrow P_{t_0}(x,\cdot)\) pointwise against bounded continuous functions, if \(\{P_{t_0}^n(x,\cdot):n\ge1,x\in K\}\) is uniformly absolutely continuous with respect to \(
u\) for every compact \(K\subset E\), and if every \(P\)-invariant probability measure absolutely continuous with respect to \(
u\) equals \(
u\), then every \(P\)-invariant probability measure equals \(
u\). It also correctly proves that a compact-local uniform \(L^p(
u)\) bound on densities implies the compact-local uniform absolute-continuity assumption. It does not prove the needed smoothing or absolutely-continuous invariant-measure uniqueness assumptions for the original singular SPDE.
selector_skipped_reason: N_PARALLEL_RUNS=1 — single run, no selection needed
